ip_intent.one_line_desc: Managing identities and root keys; shielding confidential assets from software; providing a key derivation interface for software
ip_intent.one_paragraph_desc:
Key Manager implements the hardware component of the identities and root keys strategy of OpenTitan.
It shields confidential assets from direct software access and provides an interface for software to use derived keys and identity outputs.
The key derivation functionality interfaces KMAC Accelerator.
Key Manager provides AES Accelerator, OTBN, and KMAC Accelerator with confidential keys over sideload interfaces that are not accessible from software.
In addition, it supports the [Open Profile for DICE][pigweed-open-dice].

[pigweed-open-dice]: https://pigweed.googlesource.com/open-dice/+/HEAD/docs/specification.md#Open-Profile-for-DICE

param_list.name: UseOtpSeedsInsteadOfFlash
param_list.desc:
Flag indicating whether to use the creator / owner seeds provided
via otp_key_i instead of the ones provided in flash_i. This option
can be used in integrations where that do not have an embedded flash
controller (in which case flash_i should just be tied off).

param_list.name: KmacEnMasking
param_list.desc: Flag indicating with kmac masking is enabled

param_list.name: RndCnstLfsrSeed
param_list.desc: Compile-time random bits for initial LFSR seed

param_list.name: RndCnstLfsrPerm
param_list.desc: Compile-time random permutation for LFSR output

param_list.name: RndCnstRandPerm
param_list.desc: Compile-time random permutation for entropy used in share overriding

param_list.name: RndCnstRevisionSeed
param_list.desc: Compile-time random bits for revision seed

param_list.name: RndCnstCreatorIdentitySeed
param_list.desc: Compile-time random bits for creator identity seed

param_list.name: RndCnstOwnerIntIdentitySeed
param_list.desc: Compile-time random bits for owner intermediate identity seed

param_list.name: RndCnstOwnerIdentitySeed
param_list.desc: Compile-time random bits for owner identity seed

param_list.name: RndCnstSoftOutputSeed
param_list.desc: Compile-time random bits for software generation seed

param_list.name: RndCnstHardOutputSeed
param_list.desc: Compile-time random bits for hardware generation seed

param_list.name: RndCnstAesSeed
param_list.desc: Compile-time random bits for generation seed when aes destination selected

param_list.name: RndCnstKmacSeed
param_list.desc: Compile-time random bits for generation seed when kmac destination selected

param_list.name: RndCnstOtbnSeed
param_list.desc: Compile-time random bits for generation seed when otbn destination selected

param_list.name: RndCnstCdi
param_list.desc: Compile-time random bits for generation seed when no CDI is selected

param_list.name: RndCnstNoneSeed
param_list.desc: Compile-time random bits for generation seed when no destination selected

param_list.name: NumSaltReg
param_list.desc: Number of Registers for SW inputs (Salt)

param_list.name: NumSwBindingReg
param_list.desc: Number of Registers for SW inputs (SW binding)

param_list.name: NumOutReg
param_list.desc: Number of Registers for SW outputs

param_list.name: NumKeyVersion
param_list.desc: Number of Registers for key version

alerts.name: recov_operation_err
alerts.desc:
Alert for key manager operation errors.  These errors could have been caused by
software

alerts.name: fatal_fault_err
alerts.desc: Alert for key manager faults.  These errors cannot be caused by software

fields.name: EN
fields.desc:
key manager configuration enable.
When key manager operation is started (see CONTROL), registers protected by this EN are no longer
modifiable until the operation completes.

fields.name: EN
fields.desc: Start key manager operations

fields.name: OPERATION
fields.desc: Key manager operation selection. All values not enumerated below behave the same as disable

fields.name: CDI_SEL
fields.desc:
When the OPERATION field is programmed to generate output, this field selects
the appropriate CDI to use.

This field should be programmed for both hw / sw generation.

fields.name: DEST_SEL
fields.desc:
When the OPERATION field is programmed to generate output, this field selects
the appropriate crypto cipher target.

This field should be programmed for both hw / sw generation, as this helps diversifies the output.

fields.name: VAL
fields.desc:
Depending on the value programmed, a different sideload key slot is cleared.
If the value programmed is not one of the enumerated values below, ALL sideload
key slots are continuously cleared. In order to stop continuous clearing, SW should
toggle the clear bit again (i.e. disable continuous clearing).

fields.name: EN
fields.desc: Configuration enable for reseed interval

registers.name: RESEED_INTERVAL_SHADOWED
registers.desc: Reseed interval for key manager entropy reseed

fields.name: VAL
fields.desc: Number of internal PRNG updates before a reseed is requested.

fields.name: EN
fields.desc:
Software binding register write enable.
This is locked by software and unlocked by hardware upon a successful advance call.

Software binding resets to 1, and its value cannot be altered by software until advancement to Init state.

fields.name: EN
fields.desc: MAX_CREATOR_KEY_VERSION configure enable.

fields.name: VAL
fields.desc:
Max key version.

Any key version up to the value specified in this register is valid.

fields.name: EN
fields.desc: MAX_OWNER_INTERMEDIATE_KEY configure enable.

fields.name: VAL
fields.desc:
Max key version.

Any key version up to the value specified in this register is valid.

fields.name: EN
fields.desc: MAX_OWNER_KEY configure enable.

fields.name: VAL
fields.desc:
Max key version.

Any key version up to the value specified in this register is valid.

registers.name: WORKING_STATE
registers.desc:
Key manager working state.

This is a readout of the current key manager working state

fields.name: STATE
fields.desc: Key manager control state

registers.name: OP_STATUS
registers.desc:
Key manager status.

Hardware sets the status based on software initiated operations.
This register must be explicitly cleared by software.
Software clears by writing back whatever it reads.

fields.name: STATUS
fields.desc: Operation status.

registers.name: ERR_CODE
registers.desc:
Key manager error code.
This register must be explicitly cleared by software.

This register represents both synchronous and asynchronous recoverable
errors.

Synchronous errors refer to those that only happen when a keymgr operation is
invoked, while asynchronous refers to errors that can happen at any time.

fields.name: INVALID_OP
fields.desc: Invalid operation issued to key manager, synchronous error

fields.name: INVALID_KMAC_INPUT
fields.desc: Invalid data issued to kmac interface, synchronous error

fields.name: INVALID_SHADOW_UPDATE
fields.desc: An error observed during shadow register updates, asynchronous error

registers.name: FAULT_STATUS
registers.desc:
This register represents both synchronous and asynchronous fatal faults.

Synchronous faults refer to those that only happen when a keymgr operation is
invoked, while asynchronous refers to faults that can happen at any time.

fields.name: CMD
fields.desc: A non-onehot command was seen in kmac, asynchronous fault.

fields.name: KMAC_FSM
fields.desc: The kmac transfer interface FSM is in an invalid state, asynchronous fault.

fields.name: KMAC_DONE
fields.desc: The kmac transfer interface encountered an unexpected done, asynchronous fault.

fields.name: KMAC_OP
fields.desc: KMAC reported an error during keymgr usage, this should never happen - synchronous fault.

fields.name: KMAC_OUT
fields.desc: KMAC data returned as all 0's or all 1's - synchronous fault

fields.name: REGFILE_INTG
fields.desc: Register file integrity error, asynchronous fault

fields.name: SHADOW
fields.desc: Shadow copy storage error, asynchronous fault

fields.name: CTRL_FSM_INTG
fields.desc: Control FSM integrity error, asynchronous fault

fields.name: CTRL_FSM_CHK
fields.desc: Control FSM cross check error, asynchronous fault

fields.name: CTRL_FSM_CNT
fields.desc: Control FSM counter integrity error, asynchronous fault

fields.name: RESEED_CNT
fields.desc: Reseed counter integrity error, asynchronous fault

fields.name: SIDE_CTRL_FSM
fields.desc: Sideload control FSM integrity error, asynchronous fault

fields.name: SIDE_CTRL_SEL
fields.desc: Sideload control key select error, synchronous fault

fields.name: KEY_ECC
fields.desc: Secret key ecc error, asynchronous fault

registers.name: DEBUG
registers.desc:
The register holds some debug information that may be convenient if keymgr
misbehaves.

fields.name: INVALID_CREATOR_SEED
fields.desc: Creator seed failed input checks during operation

fields.name: INVALID_OWNER_SEED
fields.desc: Owner seed failed input checks during operation

fields.name: INVALID_DEV_ID
fields.desc: Device ID failed input checks during operation

fields.name: INVALID_HEALTH_STATE
fields.desc: Health state failed input checks during operation

fields.name: INVALID_KEY_VERSION
fields.desc: Key version failed input checks during operation

fields.name: INVALID_KEY
fields.desc: Key fed to kmac failed input checks during operation

fields.name: INVALID_DIGEST
fields.desc: ROM digest failed input checks during operation
